title: view area is not adjustable when focusing on connection windows
author: hungrysnake
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
the view area is not adjustable even the mouse pointer is showing adjustable.
it happens when the foucs is on the connection (eg. putty), when put the mouse hover the edge, the cursor changed to adjustable mode but it can't adjust (slide the edge). I have to click the connection windows (left side connection tree), then the adjustment works.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42885440/155708216-182d30bc-b07f...

iterations.description: The 'SmartSize' feature, when enabled for RDP/VNC connections, prevents manual resizing of the view area; the issue likely stems from users being unable to toggle this setting or an unclear UI for adjustment when SmartSize is off.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: there is a limitation currently from older library not properly supported in .NET 6.0 issue is known keep looking for solution

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:

_Automated by mRemoteNG Issue Intelligence System_

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The 'SmartSize' feature, when enabled for RDP/VNC connections, prevents manual resizing of the view area; the issue likely stems from users being unable to toggle this setting or an unclear UI for adjustment when SmartSize is off.
Approach: Investigate the 'SmartSize' toggle functionality within ConnectionWindow.cs and its underlying implementation in RdpProtocol.cs and Connection.Protocol.VNC.cs to ensure disabling SmartSize correctly allows manual view area adjustment and that the UI clearly indicates SmartSize status and allows for intuitive toggling.
